title: Across the Great Divide: Every Noah Kahan song ranked from worst to best
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan released his fourth studio album and was featured in a Netflix documentary in 2026.
Kahan is scheduled to perform four shows at Fenway Park on July 7, 8, 10, and 11, 2026.
Boston.com ranks all 76 of Noah Kahan's songs from his studio albums and EPs.

title: Historic island house on 25 acres in Maine hits market
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Historic Gilbert Head on Long Island, Georgetown, Maine listed for $3.85 million, featuring a furnished Federal-style home.
Property includes main house, artist's studio, deep-water dock, and hiking trails on 25 acres accessible by boat.
Built in 1837, the house maintains Federal architectural style with modern amenities and retains historical charm.

title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Joseph Aoun, president of Northeastern University, reported a $3,045,428 total compensation for Fiscal Year 2025.
Aoun's compensation includes $700,000 deferred compensation and $550,000 in bonuses, surpassing other Massachusetts college presidents.
Sally Kornbluth, president of MIT, received the second-highest compensation, earning $2,151,788 in the same fiscal year.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge, Saturday morning.
The shooting occurred shortly before 4:30 a.m.; a pedestrian discovered Bautista's body around 5:30 a.m. and called 911.
Cambridge police, Massachusetts State Police, and the DA's office are investigating the fatal shooting and seeking information.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium volunteered in South Africa to help endangered penguin chicks.
Major assisted the Southern African Foundation for the Conservation of Coastal Birds in caring for 100 penguin chicks.
African penguins are critically endangered, with a 97% population decline; conservation efforts aim to avert extinction by 2035.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras is appealing a seven-game suspension for his role in a brawl with the Washington Nationals.
Two earthquakes in Venezuela, Contreras's home country, killed nearly 3,000 and deeply affected him emotionally.
Contreras apologized for recent confrontations, acknowledging the emotional strain he has faced in the past week.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez will represent the Boston Red Sox at the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman has a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts and 17 saves in 25.2 innings this season.
Ranger Suarez, with a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings, will return to Philadelphia for the game.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce encouraged Jaylen Brown to use his feelings about being traded to the 76ers as motivation.
Pierce related to Brown's situation, recalling his own trade from the Celtics to the Nets in 2013.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by Boston and plans to use this to further his career.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated July 4th with a parade and a reading of the Declaration of Independence for the 250th anniversary.
Mayor Michelle Wu participated in the festivities, which began at the Old State House and included a parade.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular featured performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper, along with a drone show.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the past 30 days.
Durbin's offensive improvement coincides with working with a new hitting coach, showing a significant performance increase.
Boston has team control of Durbin until 2031, providing roster flexibility amidst contract talks and future free agency plans.
